name: sshpubkeys
documentation:
# OpenSSH Public Key Parser for Python

```{image} https://github.com/ojarva/python-sshpubkeys/workflows/Run%20python%20tests/badge.svg
```

## Major changes between versions 2 and 3

- Dropped support for Python 2.6 and 3.3
- Even in loose mode, DSA keys must be 1024, 2048, or 3072 bits (earlier this was looser)
- The interface (API) is exactly the same

## Usage

Native implementation for validating OpenSSH public keys.

Currently ssh-rsa, ssh-dss (DSA), ssh-ed25519 and ecdsa keys with NIST curves are supported.

Installation:

```
pip install sshpubkeys
```

or clone the [repository](https://github.com/ojarva/sshpubkeys) and use

```
python setup.py install
```

Usage:

```
import sys
from sshpubkeys import SSHKey

ssh = SSHKey("ssh-rsa AAAAB3NzaC1yc2EAAAADAQABAAAAYQCxO38tKAJXIs9ivPxt7AY"
      "dfybgtAR1ow3Qkb9GPQ6wkFHQqcFDe6faKCxH6iDRteo4D8L8B"
      "xwzN42uZSB0nfmjkIxFTcEU3mFSXEbWByg78aoddMrAAjatyrh"
      "H1pON6P0= ojarva@ojar-laptop", strict=True)
try:
    ssh.parse()
except InvalidKeyError as err:
    print("Invalid key:", err)
    sys.exit(1)
except NotImplementedError as err:
    print("Invalid key type:", err)
    sys.exit(1)

print(ssh.bits)  # 768
print(ssh.hash_md5())  # 56:84:1e:90:08:3b:60:c7:29:70:5f:5e:25:a6:3b:86
print(ssh.hash_sha256())  # SHA256:xk3IEJIdIoR9MmSRXTP98rjDdZocmXJje/28ohMQEwM
print(ssh.hash_sha512())  # SHA512:1C3lNBhjpDVQe39hnyy+xvlZYU3IPwzqK1rVneGavy6O3/ebjEQSFvmeWoyMTplIanmUK1hmr9nA8Skmj516HA
print(ssh.comment)  # ojar@ojar-laptop
print(ssh.options_raw)  # None (string of optional options at the beginning of public key)
print(ssh.options)  # None (options as a dictionary, parsed and validated)
```

Parsing of `authorized_keys` files:

```
import os
from sshpubkeys import AuthorizedKeysFile
f = open(os.environ["HOME"] + "/.ssh/authorized_keys", "r")
key_file = AuthorizedKeysFile(f, strict=False)

for key in key_file.keys:
    print(key.key_type, key.bits, key.hash_sha512())
```

## Options

Set options in constructor as a keywords (i.e., `SSHKey(None, strict=False)`)

- strict: defaults to True. Disallows keys OpenSSH's ssh-keygen refuses to create. For instance, this includes DSA keys where length != 1024 bits and RSA keys shorter than 1024-bit. If set to False, tries to allow all keys OpenSSH accepts, including highly insecure 1-bit DSA keys.
- skip_option_parsing: if set to True, options string is not parsed (ssh.options_raw is populated, but ssh.options is not).
- disallow_options: if set to True, options are not allowed and it will raise an
  InvalidOptionsError.

## Exceptions

- NotImplementedError if invalid ecdsa curve or unknown key type is encountered.
- InvalidKeyError if any other error is encountered:
  : - TooShortKeyError if key is too short (\<768 bits for RSA, \<1024 for DSA, \<256 for ED25519)
    - TooLongKeyError if key is too long (>16384 for RSA, >1024 for DSA, >256 for ED25519)
    - InvalidTypeError if key type ("ssh-rsa" in above example) does not match to what is included in base64 encoded data.
    - MalformedDataError if decoding and extracting the data fails.
    - InvalidOptionsError if options string is invalid.
      : - InvalidOptionNameError if option name contains invalid characters.
          : - UnknownOptionNameError if option name is not recognized.
        - MissingMandatoryOptionValueError if option needs to have parameter, but it is absent.

## Tests

See "[tests/](https://github.com/ojarva/sshpubkeys/tree/master/tests)" folder for unit tests. Use

```
python setup.py test
```

or

```
python3 setup.py test
```

to run test suite. If you have keys that are not parsed properly, or malformed keys that raise incorrect exception, please send your *public key* to <mailto:olli@jarva.fi>, and I'll include it. Alternatively, [create a new issue](https://github.com/ojarva/sshpubkeys/issues/new) or make [a pull request](https://github.com/ojarva/sshpubkeys/compare) in github.

modules.name: sshpubkeys.keys
documentation:
Parser for ssh public keys. Currently supports ssh-rsa, ssh-dsa, ssh-ed25519 and ssh-dss keys.

import sys


key_data = open("ssh-pubkey-file.pem").read()
ssh_key = SSHKey(key_data)
try:
    ssh_key.parse()
except InvalidKeyError:
    print("Invalid key")
    sys.exit(1)
print(ssh_key.bits)
pylint:disable=line-too-long

classes.name: sshpubkeys.keys.AuthorizedKeysFile
documentation:
Represents a full authorized_keys file.

Comments and empty lines are ignored.

classes.name: sshpubkeys.keys.SSHKey

methods.name: sshpubkeys.keys.SSHKey.reset
documentation: Reset all data fields.

methods.name: sshpubkeys.keys.SSHKey.hash
documentation:
Calculate md5 fingerprint.

Deprecated, use .hash_md5() instead.

methods.name: sshpubkeys.keys.SSHKey.hash_md5
documentation:
Calculate md5 fingerprint.

Shamelessly copied from http://stackoverflow.com/questions/6682815/deriving-an-ssh-fingerprint-from-a-public-key-in-python

For specification, see RFC4716, section 4.

methods.name: sshpubkeys.keys.SSHKey.hash_sha256
documentation: Calculate sha256 fingerprint.

methods.name: sshpubkeys.keys.SSHKey.hash_sha512
documentation: Calculates sha512 fingerprint.

methods.name: sshpubkeys.keys.SSHKey._unpack_by_int
documentation: Returns a tuple with (location of next data field, contents of requested data field).

methods.name: sshpubkeys.keys.SSHKey._parse_long
documentation: Calculate two's complement.

methods.name: sshpubkeys.keys.SSHKey.decode_key
documentation: Decode base64 coded part of the key.

methods.name: sshpubkeys.keys.SSHKey.parse_options
documentation: Parses ssh options string.

methods.name: sshpubkeys.keys.SSHKey._process_ssh_rsa
documentation: Parses ssh-rsa public keys.

methods.name: sshpubkeys.keys.SSHKey._process_ssh_dss
documentation: Parses ssh-dsa public keys.

methods.name: sshpubkeys.keys.SSHKey._process_ecdsa_sha
documentation: Parses ecdsa-sha public keys.

methods.name: sshpubkeys.keys.SSHKey._process_ed25516
documentation:
Parses ed25516 keys.

There is no (apparent) way to validate ed25519 keys. This only
checks data length (256 bits), but does not try to validate
the key in any way.

methods.name: sshpubkeys.keys.SSHKey._validate_application_string
documentation:
Validates Application string.

Has to be an URL starting with "ssh:". See ssh-keygen(1).

methods.name: sshpubkeys.keys.SSHKey._process_sk_ecdsa_sha
documentation: Parses sk_ecdsa-sha public keys.

methods.name: sshpubkeys.keys.SSHKey._process_sk_ed25519
documentation: Parses sk_ed25519 public keys.

methods.name: sshpubkeys.keys.SSHKey.parse
documentation:
Validates SSH public key.

Throws exception for invalid keys. Otherwise returns None.

Populates key_type, bits and bits fields.

For rsa keys, see field "rsa" for raw public key data.
For dsa keys, see field "dsa".
For ecdsa keys, see field "ecdsa".

Represents a single SSH keypair.

ssh_key = SSHKey(key_data, strict=True)
ssh_key.parse()

strict=True (default) only allows keys ssh-keygen generates. Setting strict mode to false allows
all keys OpenSSH actually accepts, including highly insecure ones. For example, OpenSSH accepts
512-bit DSA keys and 64-bit RSA keys which are highly insecure.

modules.name: sshpubkeys.exceptions
documentation: Exceptions for sshpubkeys.
pylint:disable=line-too-long,too-many-ancestors

classes.name: sshpubkeys.exceptions.InvalidKeyException
documentation: Invalid key - something is wrong with the key, and it should not be accepted, as OpenSSH will not work with it.

classes.name: sshpubkeys.exceptions.InvalidKeyError
documentation: Invalid key - something is wrong with the key, and it should not be accepted, as OpenSSH will not work with it.

classes.name: sshpubkeys.exceptions.InvalidKeyLengthException
documentation:
Invalid key length - either too short or too long.

See also TooShortKeyException and TooLongKeyException.

classes.name: sshpubkeys.exceptions.InvalidKeyLengthError
documentation:
Invalid key length - either too short or too long.

See also TooShortKeyException and TooLongKeyException.

classes.name: sshpubkeys.exceptions.TooShortKeyException
documentation: Key is shorter than what the specification allow.

classes.name: sshpubkeys.exceptions.TooShortKeyError
documentation: Key is shorter than what the specification allows.

classes.name: sshpubkeys.exceptions.TooLongKeyException
documentation: Key is longer than what the specification allows.

classes.name: sshpubkeys.exceptions.TooLongKeyError
documentation: Key is longer than what the specification allows.

classes.name: sshpubkeys.exceptions.InvalidTypeException
documentation: Key type is invalid or unrecognized.

classes.name: sshpubkeys.exceptions.InvalidTypeError
documentation: Key type is invalid or unrecognized.

classes.name: sshpubkeys.exceptions.MalformedDataException
documentation: The key is invalid - unable to parse the data. The data may be corrupted, truncated, or includes extra content that is not allowed.

classes.name: sshpubkeys.exceptions.MalformedDataError
documentation: The key is invalid - unable to parse the data. The data may be corrupted, truncated, or includes extra content that is not allowed.

classes.name: sshpubkeys.exceptions.InvalidOptionsException
documentation: Options string is invalid: it contains invalid characters, unrecognized options, or is otherwise malformed.

classes.name: sshpubkeys.exceptions.InvalidOptionsError
documentation: Options string is invalid: it contains invalid characters, unrecognized options, or is otherwise malformed.

classes.name: sshpubkeys.exceptions.InvalidOptionNameException
documentation: Invalid option name (contains disallowed characters, or is unrecognized.).

classes.name: sshpubkeys.exceptions.InvalidOptionNameError
documentation: Invalid option name (contains disallowed characters, or is unrecognized.).

classes.name: sshpubkeys.exceptions.UnknownOptionNameException
documentation: Unrecognized option name.

classes.name: sshpubkeys.exceptions.UnknownOptionNameError
documentation: Unrecognized option name.

classes.name: sshpubkeys.exceptions.MissingMandatoryOptionValueException
documentation: Mandatory option value is missing.

classes.name: sshpubkeys.exceptions.MissingMandatoryOptionValueError
documentation: Mandatory option value is missing.
